name: Mangel Halto
description: Often rated the #1 shore snorkeling spot in Aruba for reef diversity. Mangroves act as fish nurseries — French angelfish, parrotfish, blue tang, eels, and turtles.

rockiness.beach: Minimal sand — mangrove and limestone coast; wooden stairs to water
rockiness.ocean_floor: Patch reef within meters of shore; drops to ~30 m in places 🪸

characteristics.depth_profile: Rocky and mangrove-fringed entry — no long sand beach. Shallow patch reef within meters of shore, then reef drops to roughly 30 m (100 ft) in places. Channels between mangrove roots hold juvenile fish.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Extensive — rocky shore, wooden staircases into water; water shoes recommended
characteristics.sand: Minimal — mangrove and limestone coastline rather than classic sand beach
characteristics.facilities: Minimal — benches, wooden stairs; no rentals on site
fun_facts: Often rated the #1 shore snorkeling spot in Aruba for reef diversity.
Mangroves act as fish nurseries — French angelfish, parrotfish, blue tang, eels, and turtles.
Solar-powered sea scooter tours popular when current is stronger.
safety_notes: Current varies — visit at low tide for best visibility; use guided tour if inexperienced with drift.
